steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Textual Evidence question. The claim is that the narrator "prefers her hometown to other places she has visited." The correct quotation must do two things: praise the hometown AND explicitly compare it to other places — both elements are required.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '"There is always something fresh, something to be traced or discovered..."': praises the hometown but makes no comparison to other places visited. ✗
  • Choice B — '"Two large rivers join just below the village...bordered by lovely green fields..."': describes the hometown's scenery but contains no comparison to other places. ✗
  • Choice C — '"I have had a good deal of journeying in my life...but I have never taken greater delight than in my rides and drives...within the borders of my native town."': explicitly contrasts "journeying in my life" with experiences in "my native town," proving the hometown is preferred over other places. ✓
  • Choice D — '"There is never-ending pleasure in making one's self familiar with such a region."': praises the region but makes no comparison to other places the narrator has visited. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
